scope: Current math and implementation state for the sparse-MLA shared-KV backward dKV epilogue partial reducer

layout_contract.compressed_group_destination: compressed group g writes to grad_kv[:, S + g, d]
layout_contract.head_dimension: H is a reduction dimension, not an output dimension, so final compressed dKV has no H axis.
layout_contract.dimension_d: D is an output axis. Each d lane is reduced independently; D itself is not a reduction axis.

math.desired_local_group_reduction: A[g,d] = sum_{t,k: group[t,k] = g} c[t,k,d], then atomicAdd(grad_kv[S + g, d], A[g,d])
math.what_is_already_reduced_locally: The H dimension is already reduced inside the contribution tile by tl.dot or CK/rocWMMA MFMA-style math.
math.what_is_not_yet_reduced_in_the_fast_kern: Multiple (token, selected_slot) pairs that share the same compressed group are not meaningfully reduced before global memory in the retained query-major Triton kernel.

current_fast_triton_kernel.owner: one query token x head block x selected window
current_fast_triton_kernel.read: This is the actual source-boundary sparse-attention backward kernel gate: q, dO, output, lse, kv_bank, and LI indices enter; dQ and dKV leave. It is fast before dKV writes and slow when it emits the raw many-to-one dKV atomic stream.

why_one_token_owner_has_no_local_group_r.within_one_token: For the actual LI route, selected compressed groups are effectively unique within a token/selected window, so reducing by group inside a one-token CTA has modeled reduction 1.0x.
why_one_token_owner_has_no_local_group_r.across_tokens: The useful repeats appear across a token tile, not inside one token. For token_block=32 and selected_block=512, modeled write reduction is 23.813x, but the retained fast Triton owner does not see those 32 tokens at once.
why_one_token_owner_has_no_local_group_r.not_a_triton_language_limit: Triton can express multi-token work, but serializing many tokens inside one program loses the query-major producer speed that gives the 1.96 ms no-dKV baseline.

modes.mode13: token_block=32, group_panel=32, sorted sidecar, accumulates group_acc[local_group, d] in LDS, then writes valid dKV
modes.mode14: same as mode13 but final global dKV writes replaced by checksum sink

tested_cross_token_local_reducer.math: group_acc[p,d] += sum_{token in tile} sum_{k in panel: group[token,k] = p} sum_h(scaled_dP[token,h,k] * q[token,h,d] + prob[token,h,k] * grad_output[token,h,d])
tested_cross_token_local_reducer.rejection_reason: This implements the desired cross-token group reduction mathematically, but it serializes token-tile score/prob/scaled-dP production inside one CTA and spends heavy LDS/reducer work. The final global atomics are not the wall in this rejected owner.

do_not_do_next: Do not make compressed group the outer contribution loop.
Do not add another late dKV-only reducer after materialized P/scaled-dP.
Do not use the token-tile group_acc owner as the promoted sparse-attention kernel.
current_decision.next_kernel_requirement: Preserve the H64 query-major source-boundary producer that gives ~2 ms no-dKV latency, but add a group-aware segmented dKV epilogue before global memory. The implementation must reduce across (token, selected_slot) sharing compressed group without serializing the token tile's score/prob/scaled-dP producer.
current_decision.promotion_gate: A candidate must beat the retained 11.134678840637207 ms S4096 full sparse-attention backward boundary before any 4x/8x TorchTitan e2e TFLOPs run.
